Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「大阪みどりの百選」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>
=== Facilities along the river === * {{ill|Akemigawa Elementary School|ja|八尾市立曙川東小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akemigawa Elementary School|qq=}} * Tsurumishima Shrine * {{ill|Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja|近鉄大阪線 恩智駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|qq=}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 15|ja|大阪府道15号八尾茨木線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 15|qq=}} (runs parallel to the river near Oji Station) * [[:en:Japan National Route 170|National Route 170]] Osaka Outer Loop Line * Kashiwamura Inari Shrine * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 182|ja|大阪府道182号柏村南本町線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 182|qq=}} Kashiwamura Bridge Intersection * Kongo Yao Junior and Senior High School * Genki Onsen Yao Oyu-ba (Kashiwajyu Onsen) * Seiyu Kindergarten * Osaka Gas Dormitory * {{ill|Takaoka High School|ja|大阪府立山本高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaoka High School|qq=}} * Takaoka Sakura Shopping Street * {{ill|Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|ja|八尾市立山本球場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|qq=}} * [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] Kawachi-Yamamoto Station

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamakushi River]][[ja:玉串川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11572360]] Wikidata name: Tamakushi River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox 河川 |名称= Tamakushi River (玉串川) |image= [[File:Tamakusi-river01.JPG|300px]] |image説明= Near Yamamoto Stadium in Yao City, Yamamoto-cho Minami 7-chome, September 18, 2006 |水系等級= [[:en:Classification of rivers in Japan|Class 1 Water System]] |水系= [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]] |種別= {{ill|Class 1 River (Japan)|ja|一級河川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class 1 River (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} |延長= Approximately 6.4 km |標高= -- |流量= -- |観測所= |流域面積= -- |水源= {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|qq=|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}<small> ([[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] Futamata)</small> |河口= <small>{{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|qq=|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} ([[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]] Tamakushi Motomachi)</small> |流域= [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |脚注= |References= }}
'''Tamakushi River''' (玉串川) is an {{ill|Agricultural drainage canal|ja|農業用排水路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agricultural drainage canal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Statutory public property|ja|法定外公共物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Statutory public property|qq=|1=non-statutory public property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555107}}) flowing through [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|qq=|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}, it has been selected by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries as one of the {{ill|100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja|疎水百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Irrigation Systems|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 疏水百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=八尾市内の主な河川・水路|url=https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/0000061083.html|publisher=八尾市都市整備部土木管財課|date=2021-12-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=大阪府／大和川分水築留掛かり|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/chubunm/chubu_nm/tukidome.html|publisher=大阪府|date=2020-10-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref>
== Geography == The Tamakushi River branches from the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|qq=|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} at Futamata in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], flows north through the center of Yao City, and joins the {{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|qq=|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} at [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]], Tamakushi Motomachi. The river is approximately 5 meters wide, with stone or block embankments on both sides.
== Municipalities along the river == ; [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] : [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]]
== History == The river's name, "Tamakushi," is thought to originate from an event in the 6th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (754 CE), when a sacred offering, Tamakushige, was floated down from the upper Yamatogawa to calm storms. The offering reached a pond in Tsubara (now Hanazono Hommachi), leading to the establishment of {{ill|Tsubara Shrine|ja|津原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to enshrine the deity. It is said that the region subsequently experienced no further storm damage.
During the Edo period, the river divided at the southernmost tip of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], {{ill|Wakae District|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District|qq=|1=Wakae district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616589}} (now Yao City, Futamata), into the {{ill|Kyūhōji River|ja|久宝寺川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūhōji River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長瀬川 (大阪府)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} and the {{ill|Tamakushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi River|qq=|1=Tamakushi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572360}}.
The {{ill|Yamatogawa Diversion Project|ja|大和川付替え|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatogawa Diversion Project|qq=|1=Yamatogawa Diversion Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131618748}} of 1704 (Hōei 1) caused farmland along the old riverbed to lose its water source. The former riverbed was later repurposed for irrigation, resulting in the current Nagase and Tamakushi rivers. Details of post-diversion history can be found under Nagase River.
Prior to the late 1960s, the Tamakushi River flowed further north, passing through Higashi-Osaka's Hanazono, Hishiyahigashi, Aramoto, and Shichikenya areas, eventually merging with the {{ill|Kusunone River|ja|楠根川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunone River|qq=|1=Kusune River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541228}} near Suwa in [[:en:Jōtō-ku, Osaka|Jōtō-ku, Osaka]]. In 1969 (Shōwa 44), the construction of the Second Neyagawa cut off this northern section, with most of it now filled in.
== Environmental restoration == During Japan's [[:en:Japanese economic miracle|High Economic Growth Period]], the water quality of the Tamakushi River deteriorated due to pollution from upstream sources, including the Nagase and Yamatogawa rivers. Environmental improvements included the creation of walking paths, planting of aquatic vegetation, and installation of simple aeration devices. Juvenile carp and crucian carp were also released into the river during this time.
== Scenic features == The area south of Kashiwamura in Yao City consists of expansive fields, while the surroundings of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] are commercial zones. The rest of the area is primarily quiet residential neighborhoods. Cherry trees were planted along the riverbanks in sections such as Futamata, Kashiwamura Bridge to Gogatsubashi intersection, and Yamamoto Bridge to Yamamoto-cho Kita 7-chome. These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「大阪みどりの百選」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>
=== Facilities along the river === * {{ill|Akemigawa Elementary School|ja|八尾市立曙川東小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akemigawa Elementary School|qq=|1=Akemigawa Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390823}} * Tsurumishima Shrine * {{ill|Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja|近鉄大阪線 恩智駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 15|ja|大阪府道15号八尾茨木線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 15|qq=|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 15|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441279}} (runs parallel to the river near Oji Station) * [[:en:Japan National Route 170|National Route 170]] Osaka Outer Loop Line * Kashiwamura Inari Shrine * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 182|ja|大阪府道182号柏村南本町線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 182|qq=|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 182|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441298}} Kashiwamura Bridge Intersection * Kongo Yao Junior and Senior High School * Genki Onsen Yao Oyu-ba (Kashiwajyu Onsen) * Seiyu Kindergarten * Osaka Gas Dormitory * {{ill|Takaoka High School|ja|大阪府立山本高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaoka High School|qq=|1=Osaka Prefectural Yamamoto High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441066}} * Takaoka Sakura Shopping Street * {{ill|Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|ja|八尾市立山本球場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|qq=|1=Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390815}} * [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] Kawachi-Yamamoto Station
== Gallery of cherry blossoms == <gallery> File:Tamakushi-river 2008 spring01.JPG|Yao City, Yamamoto Takayasu-cho File:Tamakushi-river2007spring.JPG|Yao City, Kashiwamura </gallery>

== Related articles == {{commonscat|Tamakushi River}} * {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}} * [[:en:List of rivers of Japan|List of rivers of Japan]]

These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「大阪みどりの百選」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>
=== Facilities along the river === * {{ill|Akemigawa Elementary School|ja|八尾市立曙川東小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akemigawa Elementary School|qq=|1=Akemigawa Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390823}} * Tsurumishima Shrine * {{ill|Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja|近鉄大阪線 恩智駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 15|ja|大阪府道15号八尾茨木線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 15|qq=|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 15|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441279}} (runs parallel to the river near Oji Station) * [[:en:Japan National Route 170|National Route 170]] Osaka Outer Loop Line * Kashiwamura Inari Shrine * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 182|ja|大阪府道182号柏村南本町線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 182|qq=|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 182|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441298}} Kashiwamura Bridge Intersection * Kongo Yao Junior and Senior High School * Genki Onsen Yao Oyu-ba (Kashiwajyu Onsen) * Seiyu Kindergarten * Osaka Gas Dormitory * {{ill|Takaoka High School|ja|大阪府立山本高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaoka High School|qq=|1=Osaka Prefectural Yamamoto High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441066}} * Takaoka Sakura Shopping Street * {{ill|Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|ja|八尾市立山本球場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|qq=|1=Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390815}} * [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] Kawachi-Yamamoto Station

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamakushi River]][[ja:玉串川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11572360]] Wikidata name: Tamakushi River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox 河川 |名称= Tamakushi River (玉串川) |image= [[File:Tamakusi-river01.JPG|300px]] |image説明= Near Yamamoto Stadium in Yao City, Yamamoto-cho Minami 7-chome, September 18, 2006 |水系等級= [[:en:Classification of rivers in Japan|Class 1 Water System]] |水系= [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]] |種別= {{ill|Class 1 River (Japan)|ja|一級河川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class 1 River (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} |延長= Approximately 6.4 km |標高= -- |流量= -- |観測所= |流域面積= -- |水源= {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|qq=|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}<small> ([[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] Futamata)</small> |河口= <small>{{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|qq=|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} ([[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]] Tamakushi Motomachi)</small> |流域= [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |脚注= |References= }}
'''Tamakushi River''' (玉串川) is an {{ill|Agricultural drainage canal|ja|農業用排水路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agricultural drainage canal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Statutory public property|ja|法定外公共物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Statutory public property|qq=|1=non-statutory public property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555107}}) flowing through [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|qq=|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}, it has been selected by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries as one of the {{ill|100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja|疎水百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Irrigation Systems|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 疏水百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=八尾市内の主な河川・水路|url=https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/0000061083.html|publisher=八尾市都市整備部土木管財課|date=2021-12-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=大阪府／大和川分水築留掛かり|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/chubunm/chubu_nm/tukidome.html|publisher=大阪府|date=2020-10-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref>
== Geography == The Tamakushi River branches from the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|qq=|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} at Futamata in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], flows north through the center of Yao City, and joins the {{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|qq=|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} at [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]], Tamakushi Motomachi. The river is approximately 5 meters wide, with stone or block embankments on both sides.
== Municipalities along the river == ; [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] : [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]]
== History == The river's name, "Tamakushi," is thought to originate from an event in the 6th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (754 CE), when a sacred offering, Tamakushige, was floated down from the upper Yamatogawa to calm storms. The offering reached a pond in Tsubara (now Hanazono Hommachi), leading to the establishment of {{ill|Tsubara Shrine|ja|津原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to enshrine the deity. It is said that the region subsequently experienced no further storm damage.
During the Edo period, the river divided at the southernmost tip of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], {{ill|Wakae District|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District|qq=|1=Wakae district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616589}} (now Yao City, Futamata), into the {{ill|Kyūhōji River|ja|久宝寺川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūhōji River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長瀬川 (大阪府)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} and the {{ill|Tamakushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi River|qq=|1=Tamakushi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572360}}.
The {{ill|Yamatogawa Diversion Project|ja|大和川付替え|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatogawa Diversion Project|qq=|1=Yamatogawa Diversion Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131618748}} of 1704 (Hōei 1) caused farmland along the old riverbed to lose its water source. The former riverbed was later repurposed for irrigation, resulting in the current Nagase and Tamakushi rivers. Details of post-diversion history can be found under Nagase River.
Prior to the late 1960s, the Tamakushi River flowed further north, passing through Higashi-Osaka's Hanazono, Hishiyahigashi, Aramoto, and Shichikenya areas, eventually merging with the {{ill|Kusunone River|ja|楠根川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunone River|qq=|1=Kusune River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541228}} near Suwa in [[:en:Jōtō-ku, Osaka|Jōtō-ku, Osaka]]. In 1969 (Shōwa 44), the construction of the Second Neyagawa cut off this northern section, with most of it now filled in.
== Environmental restoration == During Japan's [[:en:Japanese economic miracle|High Economic Growth Period]], the water quality of the Tamakushi River deteriorated due to pollution from upstream sources, including the Nagase and Yamatogawa rivers. Environmental improvements included the creation of walking paths, planting of aquatic vegetation, and installation of simple aeration devices. Juvenile carp and crucian carp were also released into the river during this time.
== Scenic features == The area south of Kashiwamura in Yao City consists of expansive fields, while the surroundings of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] are commercial zones. The rest of the area is primarily quiet residential neighborhoods. Cherry trees were planted along the riverbanks in sections such as Futamata, Kashiwamura Bridge to Gogatsubashi intersection, and Yamamoto Bridge to Yamamoto-cho Kita 7-chome. These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「100 Greens of Ōsaka」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>
=== Facilities along the river === * {{ill|Akemigawa Elementary School|ja|八尾市立曙川東小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akemigawa Elementary School|qq=|1=Akemigawa Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390823}} * Tsurumishima Shrine * {{ill|Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja|近鉄大阪線 恩智駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 15|ja|大阪府道15号八尾茨木線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 15|qq=|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 15|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441279}} (runs parallel to the river near Oji Station) * [[:en:Japan National Route 170|National Route 170]] Osaka Outer Loop Line * Kashiwamura Inari Shrine * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 182|ja|大阪府道182号柏村南本町線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 182|qq=|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 182|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441298}} Kashiwamura Bridge Intersection * Kongo Yao Junior and Senior High School * Genki Onsen Yao Oyu-ba (Kashiwajyu Onsen) * Seiyu Kindergarten * Osaka Gas Dormitory * {{ill|Takaoka High School|ja|大阪府立山本高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaoka High School|qq=|1=Osaka Prefectural Yamamoto High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441066}} * Takaoka Sakura Shopping Street * {{ill|Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|ja|八尾市立山本球場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|qq=|1=Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390815}} * [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] Kawachi-Yamamoto Station

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamakushi River]][[ja:玉串川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572360]] Wikidata name: Tamakushi River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox 河川 |名称= Tamakushi River (玉串川) |image= [[File:Tamakusi-river01.JPG|300px]] |image説明= Near Yamamoto Stadium in Yao City, Yamamoto-cho Minami 7-chome, September 18, 2006 |水系等級= [[:en:Classification of rivers in Japan|Class 1 Water System]] |水系= [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]] |種別= {{ill|Class 1 River (Japan)|ja|一級河川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class 1 River (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} |延長= Approximately 6.4 km |標高= -- |流量= -- |観測所= |流域面積= -- |水源= {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}<small> ([[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] Futamata)</small> |河口= <small>{{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} ([[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]] Tamakushi Motomachi)</small> |流域= [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |脚注= |References= }}
'''Tamakushi River''' (玉串川) is an {{ill|Agricultural drainage canal|ja|農業用排水路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agricultural drainage canal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Statutory public property|ja|法定外公共物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Statutory public property|1=non-statutory public property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555107}}) flowing through [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}, it has been selected by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries as one of the {{ill|100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja|疎水百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 疏水百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=八尾市内の主な河川・水路|url=https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/0000061083.html|publisher=八尾市都市整備部土木管財課|date=2021-12-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=大阪府／大和川分水築留掛かり|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/chubunm/chubu_nm/tukidome.html|publisher=大阪府|date=2020-10-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref>
== Geography == The Tamakushi River branches from the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} at Futamata in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], flows north through the center of Yao City, and joins the {{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} at [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]], Tamakushi Motomachi. The river is approximately 5 meters wide, with stone or block embankments on both sides.
== Municipalities along the river == ; [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] : [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]]
== History == The river's name, "Tamakushi," is thought to originate from an event in the 6th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (754 CE), when a sacred offering, Tamakushige, was floated down from the upper Yamatogawa to calm storms. The offering reached a pond in Tsubara (now Hanazono Hommachi), leading to the establishment of {{ill|Tsubara Shrine|ja|津原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to enshrine the deity. It is said that the region subsequently experienced no further storm damage.
During the Edo period, the river divided at the southernmost tip of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], {{ill|Wakae District|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District|1=Wakae district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616589}} (now Yao City, Futamata), into the {{ill|Kyūhōji River|ja|久宝寺川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūhōji River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長瀬川 (大阪府)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} and the {{ill|Tamakushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi River|1=Tamakushi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572360}}.
The {{ill|Yamatogawa Diversion Project|ja|大和川付替え|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatogawa Diversion Project|1=Yamatogawa Diversion Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131618748}} of 1704 (Hōei 1) caused farmland along the old riverbed to lose its water source. The former riverbed was later repurposed for irrigation, resulting in the current Nagase and Tamakushi rivers. Details of post-diversion history can be found under Nagase River.
Prior to the late 1960s, the Tamakushi River flowed further north, passing through Higashi-Osaka's Hanazono, Hishiyahigashi, Aramoto, and Shichikenya areas, eventually merging with the {{ill|Kusunone River|ja|楠根川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunone River|1=Kusune River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541228}} near Suwa in [[:en:Jōtō-ku, Osaka|Jōtō-ku, Osaka]]. In 1969 (Shōwa 44), the construction of the Second Neyagawa cut off this northern section, with most of it now filled in.
== Environmental restoration == During Japan's [[:en:Japanese economic miracle|High Economic Growth Period]], the water quality of the Tamakushi River deteriorated due to pollution from upstream sources, including the Nagase and Yamatogawa rivers. Environmental improvements included the creation of walking paths, planting of aquatic vegetation, and installation of simple aeration devices. Juvenile carp and crucian carp were also released into the river during this time.
== Scenic features == The area south of Kashiwamura in Yao City consists of expansive fields, while the surroundings of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] are commercial zones. The rest of the area is primarily quiet residential neighborhoods. Cherry trees were planted along the riverbanks in sections such as Futamata, Kashiwamura Bridge to Gogatsubashi intersection, and Yamamoto Bridge to Yamamoto-cho Kita 7-chome. These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「100 Greens of Ōsaka」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamakushi River]][[ja:玉串川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572360]] Wikidata name: Tamakushi River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox 河川 |名称= Tamakushi River (玉串川) |image= [[File:Tamakusi-river01.JPG|300px]] |image説明= Near Yamamoto Stadium in Yao City, Yamamoto-cho Minami 7-chome, September 18, 2006 |水系等級= [[:en:Classification of rivers in Japan|Class 1 Water System]] |水系= [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]] |種別= {{ill|Class 1 River (Japan)|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class 1 River (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} |延長= Approximately 6.4 km |標高= -- |流量= -- |観測所= |流域面積= -- |水源= {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}<small> ([[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] Futamata)</small> |河口= <small>{{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} ([[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]] Tamakushi Motomachi)</small> |流域= [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |脚注= |References= }}
'''Tamakushi River''' (玉串川) is an {{ill|Agricultural drainage canal|ja|農業用排水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agricultural drainage canal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Statutory public property|ja|法定外公共物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Statutory public property|1=non-statutory public property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555107}}) flowing through [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}, it has been selected by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries as one of the {{ill|100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja|疎水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 疏水百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=八尾市内の主な河川・水路|url=https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/0000061083.html|publisher=八尾市都市整備部土木管財課|date=2021-12-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=大阪府／大和川分水築留掛かり|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/chubunm/chubu_nm/tukidome.html|publisher=大阪府|date=2020-10-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref>
== Geography == The Tamakushi River branches from the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} at Futamata in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], flows north through the center of Yao City, and joins the {{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} at [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]], Tamakushi Motomachi. The river is approximately 5 meters wide, with stone or block embankments on both sides.
== Municipalities along the river == ; [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] : [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]]
== History == The river's name, "Tamakushi," is thought to originate from an event in the 6th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (754 CE), when a sacred offering, Tamakushige, was floated down from the upper Yamatogawa to calm storms. The offering reached a pond in Tsubara (now Hanazono Hommachi), leading to the establishment of {{ill|Tsubara Shrine|ja|津原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to enshrine the deity. It is said that the region subsequently experienced no further storm damage.
During the Edo period, the river divided at the southernmost tip of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], {{ill|Wakae District|ja|若江郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District|1=Wakae district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616589}} (now Yao City, Futamata), into the {{ill|Kyūhōji River|ja|久宝寺川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūhōji River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長瀬川 (大阪府)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} and the {{ill|Tamakushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi River|1=Tamakushi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572360}}.
The {{ill|Yamatogawa Diversion Project|ja|大和川付替え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatogawa Diversion Project|1=Yamatogawa Diversion Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131618748}} of 1704 (Hōei 1) caused farmland along the old riverbed to lose its water source. The former riverbed was later repurposed for irrigation, resulting in the current Nagase and Tamakushi rivers. Details of post-diversion history can be found under Nagase River.
Prior to the late 1960s, the Tamakushi River flowed further north, passing through Higashi-Osaka's Hanazono, Hishiyahigashi, Aramoto, and Shichikenya areas, eventually merging with the {{ill|Kusunone River|ja|楠根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunone River|1=Kusune River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541228}} near Suwa in [[:en:Jōtō-ku, Osaka|Jōtō-ku, Osaka]]. In 1969 (Shōwa 44), the construction of the Second Neyagawa cut off this northern section, with most of it now filled in.
== Environmental restoration == During Japan's [[:en:Japanese economic miracle|High Economic Growth Period]], the water quality of the Tamakushi River deteriorated due to pollution from upstream sources, including the Nagase and Yamatogawa rivers. Environmental improvements included the creation of walking paths, planting of aquatic vegetation, and installation of simple aeration devices. Juvenile carp and crucian carp were also released into the river during this time.
== Scenic features == The area south of Kashiwamura in Yao City consists of expansive fields, while the surroundings of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] are commercial zones. The rest of the area is primarily quiet residential neighborhoods. Cherry trees were planted along the riverbanks in sections such as Futamata, Kashiwamura Bridge to Gogatsubashi intersection, and Yamamoto Bridge to Yamamoto-cho Kita 7-chome. These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「100 Greens of Ōsaka」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>
=== Facilities along the river === * {{ill|Akemigawa Elementary School|ja|八尾市立曙川東小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akemigawa Elementary School|1=Akemigawa Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390823}} * Tsurumishima Shrine * {{ill|Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja|近鉄大阪線 恩智駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 15|ja|大阪府道15号八尾茨木線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 15|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 15|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441279}} (runs parallel to the river near Oji Station) * [[:en:Japan National Route 170|National Route 170]] Osaka Outer Loop Line * Kashiwamura Inari Shrine * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 182|ja|大阪府道182号柏村南本町線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 182|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 182|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441298}} Kashiwamura Bridge Intersection * Kongo Yao Junior and Senior High School * Genki Onsen Yao Oyu-ba (Kashiwajyu Onsen) * Seiyu Kindergarten * Osaka Gas Dormitory * {{ill|Takaoka High School|ja|大阪府立山本高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaoka High School|1=Osaka Prefectural Yamamoto High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441066}} * Takaoka Sakura Shopping Street * {{ill|Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|ja|八尾市立山本球場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|1=Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390815}} * [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] Kawachi-Yamamoto Station

'''Tamakushi River''' (玉串川) is an {{ill|Agricultural drainage canal|ja|農業用排水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agricultural drainage canal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Statutory public property|ja|法定外公共物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Statutory public property|1=non-statutory public property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555107}}) flowing through [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}, it has been selected by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries as one of the {{ill|100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja|疎水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 疏水百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=八尾市内の主な河川・水路|url=https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/0000061083.html|publisher=八尾市都市整備部土木管財課|date=2021-12-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=大阪府／大和川分水築留掛かり|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/chubunm/chubu_nm/tukidome.html|publisher=大阪府|date=2020-10-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref>
== Geography == The Tamakushi River branches from the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} at Futamata in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], flows north through the center of Yao City, and joins the {{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} at [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]], Tamakushi Motomachi. The river is approximately 5 meters wide, with stone or block embankments on both sides.
== Municipalities along the river == ; [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] : [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]]
== History == The river's name, "Tamakushi," is thought to originate from an event in the 6th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (754 CE), when a sacred offering, Tamakushige, was floated down from the upper Yamatogawa to calm storms. The offering reached a pond in Tsubara (now Hanazono Hommachi), leading to the establishment of {{ill|Tsubara Shrine|ja|津原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to enshrine the deity. It is said that the region subsequently experienced no further storm damage.
During the Edo period, the river divided at the southernmost tip of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], {{ill|Wakae District|ja|若江郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District|1=Wakae district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616589}} (now Yao City, Futamata), into the {{ill|Kyūhōji River|ja|久宝寺川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūhōji River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長瀬川 (大阪府)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} and the {{ill|Tamakushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi River|1=Tamakushi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572360}}.
The {{ill|Yamatogawa Diversion Project|ja|大和川付替え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatogawa Diversion Project|1=Yamatogawa Diversion Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131618748}} of 1704 (Hōei 1) caused farmland along the old riverbed to lose its water source. The former riverbed was later repurposed for irrigation, resulting in the current Nagase and Tamakushi rivers. Details of post-diversion history can be found under Nagase River.
Prior to the late 1960s, the Tamakushi River flowed further north, passing through Higashi-Osaka's Hanazono, Hishiyahigashi, Aramoto, and Shichikenya areas, eventually merging with the {{ill|Kusunone River|ja|楠根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunone River|1=Kusune River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541228}} near Suwa in [[:en:Jōtō-ku, Osaka|Jōtō-ku, Osaka]]. In 1969 (Shōwa 44), the construction of the Second Neyagawa cut off this northern section, with most of it now filled in.
== Environmental restoration == During Japan's [[:en:Japanese economic miracle|High Economic Growth Period]], the water quality of the Tamakushi River deteriorated due to pollution from upstream sources, including the Nagase and Yamatogawa rivers. Environmental improvements included the creation of walking paths, planting of aquatic vegetation, and installation of simple aeration devices. Juvenile carp and crucian carp were also released into the river during this time.
== Scenic features == The area south of Kashiwamura in Yao City consists of expansive fields, while the surroundings of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] are commercial zones. The rest of the area is primarily quiet residential neighborhoods. Cherry trees were planted along the riverbanks in sections such as Futamata, Kashiwamura Bridge to Gogatsubashi intersection, and Yamamoto Bridge to Yamamoto-cho Kita 7-chome. These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「100 Greens of Ōsaka」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>

Together with the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}, it has been selected by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries as one of the {{ill|100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja|疎水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 疏水百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=八尾市内の主な河川・水路|url=https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/0000061083.html|publisher=八尾市都市整備部土木管財課|date=2021-12-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=大阪府／大和川分水築留掛かり|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/chubunm/chubu_nm/tukidome.html|publisher=大阪府|date=2020-10-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref>
== Geography == The Tamakushi River branches from the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} at Futamata in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], flows north through the center of Yao City, and joins the {{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} at [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]], Tamakushi Motomachi. The river is approximately 5 meters wide, with stone or block embankments on both sides.
== Municipalities along the river == ; [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] : [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]]
== History == The river's name, "Tamakushi," is thought to originate from an event in the 6th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (754 CE), when a sacred offering, Tamakushige, was floated down from the upper Yamatogawa to calm storms. The offering reached a pond in Tsubara (now Hanazono Hommachi), leading to the establishment of {{ill|Tsubara Shrine|ja|津原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to enshrine the deity. It is said that the region subsequently experienced no further storm damage.
During the Edo period, the river divided at the southernmost tip of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], {{ill|Wakae District|ja|若江郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District|1=Wakae district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616589}} (now Yao City, Futamata), into the {{ill|Kyūhōji River|ja|久宝寺川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūhōji River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長瀬川 (大阪府)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} and the {{ill|Tamakushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi River|1=Tamakushi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572360}}.
The {{ill|Yamatogawa Diversion Project|ja|大和川付替え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatogawa Diversion Project|1=Yamatogawa Diversion Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131618748}} of 1704 (Hōei 1) caused farmland along the old riverbed to lose its water source. The former riverbed was later repurposed for irrigation, resulting in the current Nagase and Tamakushi rivers. Details of post-diversion history can be found under Nagase River.
Prior to the late 1960s, the Tamakushi River flowed further north, passing through Higashi-Osaka's Hanazono, Hishiyahigashi, Aramoto, and Shichikenya areas, eventually merging with the {{ill|Kusunone River|ja|楠根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunone River|1=Kusune River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541228}} near Suwa in [[:en:Jōtō-ku, Osaka|Jōtō-ku, Osaka]]. In 1969 (Shōwa 44), the construction of the Second Neyagawa cut off this northern section, with most of it now filled in.
== Environmental restoration == During Japan's [[:en:Japanese economic miracle|High Economic Growth Period]], the water quality of the Tamakushi River deteriorated due to pollution from upstream sources, including the Nagase and Yamatogawa rivers. Environmental improvements included the creation of walking paths, planting of aquatic vegetation, and installation of simple aeration devices. Juvenile carp and crucian carp were also released into the river during this time.
== Scenic features == The area south of Kashiwamura in Yao City consists of expansive fields, while the surroundings of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] are commercial zones. The rest of the area is primarily quiet residential neighborhoods. Cherry trees were planted along the riverbanks in sections such as Futamata, Kashiwamura Bridge to Gogatsubashi intersection, and Yamamoto Bridge to Yamamoto-cho Kita 7-chome. These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「100 Greens of Ōsaka」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>

It is said that the region subsequently experienced no further storm damage.
During the Edo period, the river divided at the southernmost tip of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], {{ill|Wakae District|ja|若江郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District|1=Wakae district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616589}} (now Yao City, Futamata), into the {{ill|Kyūhōji River|ja|久宝寺川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūhōji River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長瀬川 (大阪府)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} and the {{ill|Tamakushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi River|1=Tamakushi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572360}}.
The {{ill|Yamatogawa Diversion Project|ja|大和川付替え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatogawa Diversion Project|1=Yamatogawa Diversion Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131618748}} of 1704 (Hōei 1) caused farmland along the old riverbed to lose its water source. The former riverbed was later repurposed for irrigation, resulting in the current Nagase and Tamakushi rivers. Details of post-diversion history can be found under Nagase River.
Prior to the late 1960s, the Tamakushi River flowed further north, passing through Higashi-Osaka's Hanazono, Hishiyahigashi, Aramoto, and Shichikenya areas, eventually merging with the {{ill|Kusunone River|ja|楠根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunone River|1=Kusune River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541228}} near Suwa in [[:en:Jōtō-ku, Osaka|Jōtō-ku, Osaka]]. In 1969 (Shōwa 44), the construction of the Second Neyagawa cut off this northern section, with most of it now filled in.
== Environmental restoration == During Japan's [[:en:Japanese economic miracle|High Economic Growth Period]], the water quality of the Tamakushi River deteriorated due to pollution from upstream sources, including the Nagase and Yamatogawa rivers. Environmental improvements included the creation of walking paths, planting of aquatic vegetation, and installation of simple aeration devices. Juvenile carp and crucian carp were also released into the river during this time.
== Scenic features == The area south of Kashiwamura in Yao City consists of expansive fields, while the surroundings of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] are commercial zones. The rest of the area is primarily quiet residential neighborhoods. Cherry trees were planted along the riverbanks in sections such as Futamata, Kashiwamura Bridge to Gogatsubashi intersection, and Yamamoto Bridge to Yamamoto-cho Kita 7-chome. These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「100 Greens of Ōsaka」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>
=== Facilities along the river === * {{ill|Akemigawa Elementary School|ja|八尾市立曙川東小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akemigawa Elementary School|1=Akemigawa Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390823}} * Tsurumishima Shrine * {{ill|Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja|近鉄大阪線 恩智駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 15|ja|大阪府道15号八尾茨木線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 15|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 15|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441279}} (runs parallel to the river near Oji Station) * [[:en:Japan National Route 170|National Route 170]] Osaka Outer Loop Line * Kashiwamura Inari Shrine * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 182|ja|大阪府道182号柏村南本町線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 182|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 182|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441298}} Kashiwamura Bridge Intersection * Kongo Yao Junior and Senior High School * Genki Onsen Yao Oyu-ba (Kashiwajyu Onsen) * Seiyu Kindergarten * Osaka Gas Dormitory * {{ill|Takaoka High School|ja|大阪府立山本高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaoka High School|1=Osaka Prefectural Yamamoto High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441066}} * Takaoka Sakura Shopping Street * {{ill|Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|ja|八尾市立山本球場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|1=Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390815}} * [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] Kawachi-Yamamoto Station

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamakushi River]][[ja:玉串川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572360]] Wikidata name: Tamakushi River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox 河川 |名称= Tamakushi River (玉串川) |image= [[File:Tamakusi-river01.JPG|300px]] |image説明= Near Yamamoto Stadium in Yao City, Yamamoto-cho Minami 7-chome, September 18, 2006 |水系等級= [[:en:Classification of rivers in Japan|Class 1 Water System]] |水系= [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]] |種別= {{ill|Class 1 River (Japan)|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class 1 River (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} |延長= Approximately 6.4 km |標高= -- |流量= -- |観測所= |流域面積= -- |水源= {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}<small> ([[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] Futamata)</small> |河口= <small>{{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} ([[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]] Tamakushi Motomachi)</small> |流域= [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |脚注= |References= }}
'''Tamakushi River''' (玉串川) is an {{ill|Agricultural drainage canal|ja|農業用排水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agricultural drainage canal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Statutory public property|ja|法定外公共物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Statutory public property|1=non-statutory public property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555107}}) flowing through [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}, it has been selected by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries as one of the {{ill|100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja|疎水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 疏水百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=八尾市内の主な河川・水路|url=https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/0000061083.html|publisher=八尾市都市整備部土木管財課|date=2021-12-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=大阪府／大和川分水築留掛かり|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/chubunm/chubu_nm/tukidome.html|publisher=大阪府|date=2020-10-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref>
== Geography == The Tamakushi River branches from the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} at Futamata in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], flows north through the center of Yao City, and joins the {{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} at [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]], Tamakushi Motomachi. The river is approximately 5 meters wide, with stone or block embankments on both sides.
== Municipalities along the river == ; [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] : [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]]
== History == The river's name, "Tamakushi," is thought to originate from an event in the 6th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (754 CE), when a sacred offering, Tamakushige, was floated down from the upper Yamatogawa to calm storms. The offering reached a pond in Tsubara (now Hanazono Hommachi), leading to the establishment of {{ill|Tsubara Shrine|ja|津原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to enshrine the deity. It is said that the region subsequently experienced no further storm damage.
During the Edo period, the river divided at the southernmost tip of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], {{ill|Wakae District|ja|若江郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District|1=Wakae district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616589}} (now Yao City, Futamata), into the {{ill|Kyūhōji River|ja|久宝寺川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūhōji River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長瀬川 (大阪府)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} and the {{ill|Tamakushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi River|1=Tamakushi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572360}}.
The {{ill|Yamatogawa Diversion Project|ja|大和川付替え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatogawa Diversion Project|1=Yamatogawa Diversion Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131618748}} of 1704 (Hōei 1) caused farmland along the old riverbed to lose its water source. The former riverbed was later repurposed for irrigation, resulting in the current Nagase and Tamakushi rivers. Details of post-diversion history can be found under Nagase River.
Prior to the late 1960s, the Tamakushi River flowed further north, passing through Higashi-Osaka's Hanazono, Hishiyahigashi, Aramoto, and Shichikenya areas, eventually merging with the {{ill|Kusunone River|ja|楠根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunone River|1=Kusune River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541228}} near Suwa in [[:en:Jōtō-ku, Osaka|Jōtō-ku, Osaka]]. In 1969 (Shōwa 44), the construction of the Second Neyagawa cut off this northern section, with most of it now filled in.
== Environmental restoration == During Japan's [[:en:Japanese economic miracle|High Economic Growth Period]], the water quality of the Tamakushi River deteriorated due to pollution from upstream sources, including the Nagase and Yamatogawa rivers. Environmental improvements included the creation of walking paths, planting of aquatic vegetation, and installation of simple aeration devices. Juvenile carp and crucian carp were also released into the river during this time.
== Scenic features == The area south of Kashiwamura in Yao City consists of expansive fields, while the surroundings of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] are commercial zones. The rest of the area is primarily quiet residential neighborhoods. Cherry trees were planted along the riverbanks in sections such as Futamata, Kashiwamura Bridge to Gogatsubashi intersection, and Yamamoto Bridge to Yamamoto-cho Kita 7-chome. These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「100 Greens of Ōsaka」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>
=== Facilities along the river === * {{ill|Akemigawa Elementary School|ja|八尾市立曙川東小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akemigawa Elementary School|1=Akemigawa Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390823}} * Tsurumishima Shrine * {{ill|Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja|近鉄大阪線 恩智駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 15|ja|大阪府道15号八尾茨木線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 15|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 15|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441279}} (runs parallel to the river near Oji Station) * [[:en:Japan National Route 170|National Route 170]] Osaka Outer Loop Line * Kashiwamura Inari Shrine * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 182|ja|大阪府道182号柏村南本町線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 182|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 182|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441298}} Kashiwamura Bridge Intersection * Kongo Yao Junior and Senior High School * Genki Onsen Yao Oyu-ba (Kashiwajyu Onsen) * Seiyu Kindergarten * Osaka Gas Dormitory * {{ill|Takaoka High School|ja|大阪府立山本高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaoka High School|1=Osaka Prefectural Yamamoto High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441066}} * Takaoka Sakura Shopping Street * {{ill|Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|ja|八尾市立山本球場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|1=Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390815}} * [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] Kawachi-Yamamoto Station

These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「100 Greens of Ōsaka」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>
=== Facilities along the river === * {{ill|Akemigawa Elementary School|ja|八尾市立曙川東小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akemigawa Elementary School|1=Akemigawa Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390823}} * Tsurumishima Shrine * {{ill|Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja|近鉄大阪線 恩智駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 15|ja|大阪府道15号八尾茨木線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 15|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 15|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441279}} (runs parallel to the river near Oji Station) * [[:en:Japan National Route 170|National Route 170]] Osaka Outer Loop Line * Kashiwamura Inari Shrine * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 182|ja|大阪府道182号柏村南本町線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 182|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 182|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441298}} Kashiwamura Bridge Intersection * Kongo Yao Junior and Senior High School * Genki Onsen Yao Oyu-ba (Kashiwajyu Onsen) * Seiyu Kindergarten * Osaka Gas Dormitory * {{ill|Takaoka High School|ja|大阪府立山本高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaoka High School|1=Osaka Prefectural Yamamoto High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441066}} * Takaoka Sakura Shopping Street * {{ill|Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|ja|八尾市立山本球場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|1=Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390815}} * [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] Kawachi-Yamamoto Station

<!--Tamagushi River--> [[Category:Drainage canals in Japan]] [[Category:Cherry blossom avenues]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Yodo River]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Tamakushi River}} {{wikidata link|Q11572360}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamakushi River]][[ja:玉串川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamakushi River]][[ja:玉串川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamakushi River]][[ja:玉串川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572360]] Wikidata name: Tamakushi River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox 河川 |名称= Tamakushi River (玉串川) |image= [[File:Tamakusi-river01.JPG|300px]] |image説明= Near Yamamoto Stadium in Yao City, Yamamoto-cho Minami 7-chome, September 18, 2006 |水系等級= [[:en:Classification of rivers in Japan|Class 1 Water System]] |水系= [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]] |種別= {{ill|Class 1 River (Japan)|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class 1 River (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} |延長= Approximately 6.4 km |標高= -- |流量= -- |観測所= |流域面積= -- |水源= {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}<small> ([[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]] Futamata)</small> |河口= <small>{{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} ([[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]] Tamakushi Motomachi)</small> |流域= [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |脚注= |References= }}
'''Tamakushi River''' (玉串川) is an {{ill|Agricultural drainage canal|ja|農業用排水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agricultural drainage canal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Statutory public property|ja|法定外公共物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Statutory public property|1=non-statutory public property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555107}}) flowing through [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}}, it has been selected by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries as one of the {{ill|100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja|疎水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Irrigation Systems|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 疏水百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=八尾市内の主な河川・水路|url=https://www.city.yao.osaka.jp/0000061083.html|publisher=八尾市都市整備部土木管財課|date=2021-12-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|ja|title=大阪府／大和川分水築留掛かり|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/chubunm/chubu_nm/tukidome.html|publisher=大阪府|date=2020-10-09|accessdate=2022-11-17}}</ref>
== Geography == The Tamakushi River branches from the {{ill|Nagase River (Osaka)|ja|長瀬川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River (Osaka)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} at Futamata in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], flows north through the center of Yao City, and joins the {{ill|Second Neyagawa|ja|第二寝屋川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Neyagawa|1=Daini Neya River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602287}} at [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]], Tamakushi Motomachi. The river is approximately 5 meters wide, with stone or block embankments on both sides.
== Municipalities along the river == ; [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] : [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashi-Osaka]]
== History == The river's name, "Tamakushi," is thought to originate from an event in the 6th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (754 CE), when a sacred offering, Tamakushige, was floated down from the upper Yamatogawa to calm storms. The offering reached a pond in Tsubara (now Hanazono Hommachi), leading to the establishment of {{ill|Tsubara Shrine|ja|津原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to enshrine the deity. It is said that the region subsequently experienced no further storm damage.
During the Edo period, the river divided at the southernmost tip of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], {{ill|Wakae District|ja|若江郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District|1=Wakae district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616589}} (now Yao City, Futamata), into the {{ill|Kyūhōji River|ja|久宝寺川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūhōji River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 長瀬川 (大阪府)|1=Nagase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653384}} and the {{ill|Tamakushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi River|1=Tamakushi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572360}}.
The {{ill|Yamatogawa Diversion Project|ja|大和川付替え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatogawa Diversion Project|1=Yamatogawa Diversion Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131618748}} of 1704 (Hōei 1) caused farmland along the old riverbed to lose its water source. The former riverbed was later repurposed for irrigation, resulting in the current Nagase and Tamakushi rivers. Details of post-diversion history can be found under Nagase River.
Prior to the late 1960s, the Tamakushi River flowed further north, passing through Higashi-Osaka's Hanazono, Hishiyahigashi, Aramoto, and Shichikenya areas, eventually merging with the {{ill|Kusunone River|ja|楠根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunone River|1=Kusune River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541228}} near Suwa in [[:en:Jōtō-ku, Osaka|Jōtō-ku, Osaka]]. In 1969 (Shōwa 44), the construction of the Second Neyagawa cut off this northern section, with most of it now filled in.
== Environmental restoration == During Japan's [[:en:Japanese economic miracle|High Economic Growth Period]], the water quality of the Tamakushi River deteriorated due to pollution from upstream sources, including the Nagase and Yamatogawa rivers. Environmental improvements included the creation of walking paths, planting of aquatic vegetation, and installation of simple aeration devices. Juvenile carp and crucian carp were also released into the river during this time.
== Scenic features == The area south of Kashiwamura in Yao City consists of expansive fields, while the surroundings of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] are commercial zones. The rest of the area is primarily quiet residential neighborhoods. Cherry trees were planted along the riverbanks in sections such as Futamata, Kashiwamura Bridge to Gogatsubashi intersection, and Yamamoto Bridge to Yamamoto-cho Kita 7-chome. These trees, planted starting in January 1965 through community efforts and donations, bloom annually from late March to early April.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|author=Yao City Charm Book Committee|title=Wao!Yao! Entrance to Yao|publisher=140B|date=2013-03-27}}</ref> This cherry blossom row was selected for the {{ill|100 Green Spots of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Spots of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/25974/00195639/hyakusenn.pdf|title=「100 Greens of Ōsaka」|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2018-03-29}}</ref>
=== Facilities along the river === * {{ill|Akemigawa Elementary School|ja|八尾市立曙川東小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akemigawa Elementary School|1=Akemigawa Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390823}} * Tsurumishima Shrine * {{ill|Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja|近鉄大阪線 恩智駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oji Station (Kintetsu Osaka Line)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 15|ja|大阪府道15号八尾茨木線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 15|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 15|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441279}} (runs parallel to the river near Oji Station) * [[:en:Japan National Route 170|National Route 170]] Osaka Outer Loop Line * Kashiwamura Inari Shrine * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Road 182|ja|大阪府道182号柏村南本町線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Road 182|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 182|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441298}} Kashiwamura Bridge Intersection * Kongo Yao Junior and Senior High School * Genki Onsen Yao Oyu-ba (Kashiwajyu Onsen) * Seiyu Kindergarten * Osaka Gas Dormitory * {{ill|Takaoka High School|ja|大阪府立山本高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaoka High School|1=Osaka Prefectural Yamamoto High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441066}} * Takaoka Sakura Shopping Street * {{ill|Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|ja|八尾市立山本球場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|1=Yamamoto Baseball Stadium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390815}} * [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] Kawachi-Yamamoto Station
